title: learn:execute plan/phase upgrade cannot be persisted with current ccw learn:* API
description: learn:execute.md claims legacy plan upgrades (adding phases/phase fields) will be persisted via ccw learn:update-progress, but update-progress only reads plan.json from disk and cannot accept arbitrary plan patches. This makes the documented upgrade path non-functional and risks phase gating silently not taking effect.
proposed_fix: Add a validated+lock-protected API (e.g. ccw learn:write-plan / learn:update-plan) or ensure learn:plan always outputs phases and remove upgrade logic from execute.

title: Shell injection / quoting risks in learn:execute and learn:profile CLI invocations
description: Docs interpolate user-controlled strings into Bash commands (sessionId, evidence JSON, background text) without robust quoting/escaping. Shell interpolation occurs before the ccw CLI validates IDs, creating command injection and parsing failure risk.
proposed_fix: Centralize safe CLI arg building (escapeForSingleQuotes or file-based inputs). Prefer passing large JSON via temp files/base64. Quote every interpolated arg.

title: learn:plan uses non-portable shell commands (cat/mv) in doc-as-runtime code
description: learn:plan.md uses Bash('cat ...') and Bash(`mv ...`) for critical steps (loading agent template, promoting plan.tmp.json). This can fail on non-POSIX shells or restricted environments.
proposed_fix: Replace cat/mv with Read()/Write() or node fs (renameSync) to keep the workflow portable and sandbox-friendly.

title: learn:plan ensurePhases fallback violates schema (phase_name enum)
description: assignPhasesFallback() sets phase_name to 'Phase 1', but learn-plan.schema.json restricts phase_name to a fixed enum (Foundation/Core Concepts/Advanced Topics/Specialization/Mastery). If fallback is used, schema validation blocks plan creation.
proposed_fix: Map fallback names to schema enum values (e.g. Foundation for phase 1) and add a validator test for fallback output.

title: learn-planning-agent contract mismatches learn:plan caller context (goal vs learning_goal, session_folder)
description: learn-planning-agent.md documents inputs as learning_goal and session_folder, while learn:plan builds INPUT_CONTEXT_JSON with { goal, profile, gap_analysis } and omits session_folder. This can reduce determinism and increase hallucinated fields.
proposed_fix: Unify names (goal->learning_goal) and remove session_folder requirement from agent contract (caller owns persistence). Add a test asserting contract alignment.

title: learn:plan Execution Process section drifts from implementation (Task vs ccw cli)
description: The flow overview claims Task(tool="learn-planning-agent") invocation, but the implementation uses ccw cli -p with retries and robust JSON parsing. This drift increases maintenance cost and confusion.
proposed_fix: Update the Execution Process to reflect the ccw cli invocation path (or implement and test the Task path if desired).

title: learn:update-progress writes progress.json without schema validation
description: Progress is written with atomicWriteJson(..., () => true). Without a schema, progress can drift and consumers may fail unexpectedly.
proposed_fix: Introduce learn-progress.schema.json and validate progress writes; add tests for required fields and status values.

title: learn:update-progress does not keep progress convenience arrays consistent for skipped/optional/pending
description: completed_knowledge_points/in_progress_knowledge_points are only updated for status===completed or in_progress. Other statuses remain stale and can mislead dashboards/agents.
proposed_fix: Either maintain arrays for all statuses (including skipped/optional) or remove arrays and derive views from knowledge_point_progress.

title: Learn lock has no stale-lock recovery; 2s timeout can permanently block after crash
description: withLearnLock uses an exclusive lock file and times out after 2 seconds without checking PID liveness or lock age. A crash can leave a lock file that blocks all operations until manual deletion.
proposed_fix: Record pid+timestamp, detect stale locks (pid not running / age > TTL), and implement a safe --force-unlock option.

title: learn-plan-validator does not verify prerequisites vs dependency_graph consistency
description: Validator checks dependency_graph edges/nodes and cycles but does not ensure knowledge_points[].prerequisites matches edges or references valid KP IDs.
proposed_fix: Add a validation layer that cross-checks prerequisites with dependency_graph and enforces that all prerequisite IDs exist and are acyclic.

title: learn:execute allowed-tools surface is broader than necessary (Read/Write)
description: learn:execute.md includes Read/Write in allowed-tools even though it states agents should not manually edit .workflow/learn. Reducing tool surface helps enforce the intended boundary.
proposed_fix: Remove Read/Write from allowed-tools or strictly scope allowed write locations (e.g. scratchpad only).

title: learn:plan flag normalization risk (flags.noAgent vs --no-agent)
description: learn:plan.md uses flags.noAgent but UI/argument-hint uses --no-agent. If runner doesn't normalize to camelCase, the wrong planning path may execute.
proposed_fix: Normalize flags in code (noAgent = flags.noAgent ?? flags['no-agent']) and add a doc contract test for it.
